peer-reviewed-empirical.classification_signals: Methods section, results section, statistical analysis, peer review indicators, novel data or experiments
peer-reviewed-empirical.step3_focus: Research design, quantum algorithm(s) used, hardware (simulator vs. real QPU), dataset, evaluation metrics, baseline comparisons
peer-reviewed-empirical.step4_focus: Quantified results with confidence intervals. Flag quantum advantage claims. Note simulation vs. real hardware
peer-reviewed-empirical.step5_focus: Internal validity, hardware noise, qubit count constraints, dataset size, scalability, reproducibility

peer-reviewed-theoretical.classification_signals: Formal proofs, propositions, theoretical framework, no experimental section or simulated only
peer-reviewed-theoretical.step3_focus: Theoretical framework, formal model, proof technique, assumptions, propositions
peer-reviewed-theoretical.step4_focus: Key propositions or theorems, claimed complexity advantages, conditions under which claims hold
peer-reviewed-theoretical.step5_focus: Assumptions required, gap between theoretical and practical performance, missing empirical validation

preprint.classification_signals: arXiv, SSRN, or similar preprint server DOI; no journal assignment; may lack peer review statement
preprint.step3_focus: Treat as empirical or theoretical based on content. Flag as preprint in frontmatter
preprint.step4_focus: Default quantum advantage claims to [speculative] unless strong empirical support is present
preprint.step5_focus: Same as empirical/theoretical; additionally flag lack of peer review

conference-paper.classification_signals: Conference proceedings, workshop paper, symposium presentation
conference-paper.step3_focus: Same as empirical/theoretical; note if short paper or workshop paper
conference-paper.step5_focus: Same as empirical/theoretical; note page-limit constraints

review-article.classification_signals: Explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria, PRISMA or similar protocol, synthesis of existing literature
review-article.step3_focus: Search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria, number of papers included, synthesis method, classification taxonomy
review-article.step4_focus: Key themes identified, consensus findings, areas of disagreement across reviewed literature
review-article.step5_focus: Search coverage gaps, recency, language bias, grey literature exclusion

technical-report.classification_signals: Government, standards body, or institutional authorship; technical depth without academic peer review
technical-report.step3_focus: Technical specifications, standards referenced, scope of analysis
technical-report.step5_focus: Scope constraints, institutional mandate limitations, currency of data

industry-whitepaper.classification_signals: No methods section, vendor or organisation authorship, advocacy tone, limited citations
industry-whitepaper.step3_focus: Claimed approach or framework. Note absence of formal methods section. Identify supporting evidence
industry-whitepaper.step4_focus: Claims made. Default all to [speculative] unless backed by cited peer-reviewed evidence. Note vendor context
industry-whitepaper.step5_focus: Lack of peer review, potential vendor bias, missing reproducibility, proprietary data

industry-report.classification_signals: Market research, consulting firm, or industry body authorship; data often proprietary
industry-report.step3_focus: Market methodology, data sources, analytical framework
industry-report.step4_focus: Market findings, trend claims, projections. Note proprietary data context
industry-report.step5_focus: Proprietary methodology, potential bias, limited reproducibility

other.classification_signals: Does not fit the above categories
other.step3_focus: Extract whatever methodology is present. Note unusual format
other.step4_focus: Extract findings as described. Note any context that affects reliability
other.step5_focus: Note any limitations apparent from the format or source
